beats.caption: Armstrong: That’s one small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.
Armstrong: It has a stark beauty all its own. It’s like much of the high desert of the United States — different, but very pretty out here.
Aldrin: Are you ready for me to come out?  Armstrong: Yeah. Just stand by a second.
Armstrong: All set. O.K., you’re right at the edge of the porch.
Aldrin: O.K., I’m on the top step. (Buzz Aldrin climbs down to join Armstrong on the surface.)
Armstrong: Isn’t that something! Magnificent sight out here.  Aldrin: Magnificent desolation.
Houston: They’ve got the flag up now, and you can see the stars and stripes on the lunar surface.
Aldrin: As I look around the area, the contrast, in general, comes about completely by virtue of the shadows.
Aldrin: The crater right in front of me, off about the 11 o’clock position, is about 30 to 35 feet across.
Aldrin: How’s the bulk sample coming, Neil?  Armstrong: Bulk sample is just being sealed.
